title: Template
description: A Day Plan template for NoteTaker. Stored as an array in templates.json inside the user's storage directory. Each template defines the section layout that is instantiated when the user creates a new Day Plan.

SectionType.description:
Determines which editor component renders this section and what its `items` type will be at runtime.

• text        — a freeform rich-text area. No items.
• priorities  — a numbered priority list (P1/P2/P3). Items are PriorityItem.
• checklist   — a simple checked/unchecked task list. Items are ChecklistItem.
• timeblocks  — a time-grid schedule. Items are TimeBlock.

SectionConfig.description: Optional hints used when instantiating a section from this definition. All fields are optional.

count.description: Number of blank items to pre-populate when this section is first created. Applies to priorities, checklist, and timeblocks.

defaultItems.description: Text labels for the initial items. If provided, overrides `count`. Applies to priorities and checklist.

startTime.description: Start time for the timeblocks grid in HH:MM (24-hour) format.

endTime.description: End time for the timeblocks grid in HH:MM (24-hour) format.

interval.description: Time slot duration in minutes for the timeblocks grid.

TemplateSectionDef.description: Defines one section inside a template.

key.description: Unique identifier for this section within the template. Used as the React key and for section lookup.

label.description: Display heading shown at the top of the section card.

description.description: Subtitle shown beneath the section label to guide the user.
examples: What are the three most important things to accomplish today?

schemaVersion.description:
Schema version for this template object. Omitting this field is equivalent to version 1. Increment this when the shape of the template object changes in a breaking way so the app can run the appropriate migration.

Version history:
  1 — initial shape: id, name, isBuiltIn, description, color?, sections (text | priorities | checklist | timeblocks).

id.description: Unique, stable identifier. Use kebab-case. Built-in templates use the prefix 'built-in-'.

name.description: Display name shown in the template picker.

isBuiltIn.description: When true the template ships with the app and cannot be deleted by the user.

description.description: One-sentence summary of what this template is designed for.

color.description: Optional accent color for this template's card in the picker. Any valid CSS color value.

sections.description: Ordered list of section definitions that make up the template. At least one section is required.

examples.name: Productive Day
examples.description: A balanced daily planning template with priorities, schedule, and habit tracking.

sections.label: 🎯 Top Priorities
sections.description: The three most important outcomes for today.

sections.label: 📅 Time Blocks
sections.description: Block your day hour by hour.

sections.label: ✅ Task List
sections.description: Everything else that needs to get done.

sections.label: 📝 Notes
sections.description: Thoughts, ideas, or anything worth capturing.

examples.name: Deep Work
examples.description: Minimal template focused on a single deep-work session.

sections.label: 🎯 Today's Intention
sections.description: One sentence: what does success look like at the end of this session?

sections.label: ⏱ Focus Blocks
sections.description: Map your deep work and breaks in 90-minute slots.

sections.label: 🚧 Potential Blockers
sections.description: Identify and resolve distractions before they happen.

sections.label: 💬 End-of-Day Reflection
sections.description: What went well? What would you do differently?

examples.name: Weekly Review
examples.description: A structured Friday review to close the week and plan the next.

sections.label: 🔄 Carried Over
sections.description: Tasks that didn't make it — decide: complete, delegate, or drop.

sections.label: 📚 Learnings
sections.description: What did you learn this week, personally or professionally?

sections.label: 🗓 Next Week's Priorities
sections.description: The three outcomes that matter most next week.

sections.label: ⚡ Energy & Health Notes
sections.description: Sleep, exercise, mood — track what affects your performance.
